title: Building a File-Based CRUD System
body:
Before introducing a database, it is useful to understand how data persistence works by manually reading and writing files. In a file-based system, each article can be stored as a JSON file inside a directory.

Creating an article means generating a JSON object and writing it to disk. Reading involves scanning the directory, reading each file, and parsing its contents. Updating requires overwriting the existing file, while deleting simply removes it from the filesystem.

Although this approach does not scale for large applications, it helps developers clearly understand data flow, file operations, and how backends manage state before abstracting everything behind an ORM or database layer.",
